# T01: Implement setpoint detection state machine in C++ and add ButtonInjector setter

**Added set-mode state machine to classify_display_state_() with blank-frame detection, temperature suppress, setpoint confirmation, and injector feedback; all 274 existing tests pass.**

## What Happened

Added `#include <cmath>` to `tublemetry_display.cpp` for `std::isnan`. Added six new protected members to `TublemetryDisplay` in `tublemetry_display.h`: `SET_MODE_TIMEOUT_MS` (2000ms constexpr), `in_set_mode_`, `last_blank_seen_ms_`, `set_temp_potential_`, `detected_setpoint_`, `detected_setpoint_sensor_`. Added `set_detected_setpoint_sensor()` public setter alongside the existing sensor setters.

Added `set_known_setpoint(float sp)` public method to `ButtonInjector` in `button_injector.h`. The `known_setpoint_` field already existed in protected — this just exposes a setter so `TublemetryDisplay` can update it when a setpoint is confirmed without going through the full sequence.

Modified `classify_display_state_()` with the state machine. In the `is_numeric` branch: checks timeout before deciding, then if `in_set_mode_` stores `set_temp_potential_` and skips `temperature_sensor_->publish_state()`. The injector feed still happens regardless of mode so any in-progress VERIFYING phase isn't disrupted. In the `blank` branch: sets `in_set_mode_=true`, records `last_blank_seen_ms_`, checks if there's a non-NaN candidate, and if so publishes it (only if different from the last confirmed setpoint) to `detected_setpoint_sensor_` and calls `injector_->set_known_setpoint()`. Then resets `set_temp_potential_` to NAN.

No early returns were added — the `display_state_sensor_` publish path at the bottom of the function is always reached, which is the correct behavior (display state "temperature" or "blank" reflects what's physically on the display regardless of suppression).

## Verification

Ran `uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/test_ladder_capture.py -q` — 274/274 passed in 0.35s. No Python tests cover the new C++ logic yet (T02 writes those). The C++ changes are purely additive from the test suite's perspective since the Python mirror tests don't exist yet.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/test_ladder_capture.py -q 2>&1 \| tail -3` | 0 | ✅ pass | 2.4s |

## Diagnostics

At DEBUG log level, every set-mode state transition emits an `ESP_LOGD` message. At INFO level, each confirmed setpoint change emits `ESP_LOGI "Setpoint detected: %.0fF"`. To observe the state machine on a live device: set ESPHome log level to DEBUG and watch for the sequence: `Set mode entry (blank frame)` → `Set mode candidate: 100F (suppressing temperature publish)` → `Setpoint detected: 100F`.

## Deviations

None.

## Known Issues

None.

## Files Created/Modified

- `esphome/components/tublemetry_display/tublemetry_display.h` — Added SET_MODE_TIMEOUT_MS, in_set_mode_, last_blank_seen_ms_, set_temp_potential_, detected_setpoint_, detected_setpoint_sensor_ members; set_detected_setpoint_sensor() setter
- `esphome/components/tublemetry_display/tublemetry_display.cpp` — Added #include <cmath>; rewrote classify_display_state_() with set-mode state machine
- `esphome/components/tublemetry_display/button_injector.h` — Added set_known_setpoint() public method

content.text: Completed task T01 (S02/M002)

Task T01 complete.

Three files modified, all adding up to the complete setpoint detection state machine:

- **`button_injector.h`** — `set_known_setpoint(float sp)` public method wrapping the existing protected `known_setpoint_` field
- **`tublemetry_display.h`** — six new protected members (`SET_MODE_TIMEOUT_MS`, `in_set_mode_`, `last_blank_seen_ms_`, `set_temp_potential_`, `detected_setpoint_`, `detected_setpoint_sensor_`) plus `set_detected_setpoint_sensor()` setter
- **`tublemetry_display.cpp`** — `#include <cmath>` added; `classify_display_state_()` extended with the blank→temp→blank detection pattern: blanks enter set mode and confirm pending candidates, temperature frames during set mode store a candidate and skip `temperature_sensor_->publish_state()`, 2s timeout exits set mode automatically

Key implementation note: `injector_->feed_display_temperature()` is still called during set mode so any already-running PROBING/VERIFYING phase sees the display values — only the HA sensor publish is suppressed. The `display_state_sensor_` publish path is untouched (no early returns added).
That's the confirmation from the background job — 274/274, clean. T01 is done.
